mechanism_narrative: A persistent increase in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate income and consumption, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return following month-over-month PAYEMS increases is not positive in more than 50% of out-of-sample observations, or the Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.0%, the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows becomes high enough to compress valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%, or the mean return in triggered periods is not statistically lower than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the federal funds rate and the unemployment rate signal a policy-induced slowdown or stagflation risk that is not captured by either series alone, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition does not produce negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term's incremental R-squared over main effects is less than 1% in a 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, month-over-month growth in retail sales serves as a confirming signal that consumer-driven demand remains resilient enough to support earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns following RSAFS increases occur less frequently than negative returns during periods classified as late-cycle expansion, or the hit rate is below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and operating leverage, which boosts corporate margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in INDPRO do not coincide with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of instances, or the Rank IC over a 3-year window is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts signal weakening residential investment and forward-looking consumer wealth effects, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns follow HOUST declines in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or the mean return in those periods is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the consumer price index above trend signals accelerating inflation that erodes real earnings and prompts aggressive Fed tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are not negative in at least 55% of instances when CPIAUCSL accelerates above 4.0% annualized, or the Sharpe ratio of the signal is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP signals the underlying pace of domestic economic activity and corporate profit generation, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 surprises do not coincide with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of observations, or the Rank IC over a 3-year window is below 0.03.
